title: A focus on unexpected surprises in RiPP natural product biosynthesis: Thuricin CD characterization

thuricin_cd_overview.description: Two-component sactipeptide antibiotic with unexpected enzyme cooperation mechanisms
thuricin_cd_overview.significance: Revealed unexpected surprises expanding understanding of RiPP biosynthetic mechanisms
thuricin_cd_overview.key_modification: Nested thioether rings linking cysteine residues to alpha-carbons

gene_cluster_composition.structure: Encodes for two precursor peptides and two radical S-adenosyl methionine (rSAM) enzymes

TrnA_precursor.description: Precursor peptide for thuricin CD component A

TrnB_precursor.description: Precursor peptide for thuricin CD component B

rSAM_enzyme_mechanism.reaction_mechanism: dAdoc triggers oxidative formation of nested thioether rings

initial_assumption.hypothesis: Each thuricin CD rSAM enzyme would modify specific precursor sactipeptide

surprising_discovery_1.observation: Individual TrnC or TrnD enzymes unable to modify either TrnA or TrnB
experimental_evidence: In vivo heterologous expression experiments in E. coli
In vitro enzymatic assays with purified components
surprising_discovery_1.note: Both enzymes could still cleave SAM into 5'-deoxyadenosine (typical side reaction)
surprising_discovery_1.result: No thioether moieties installed by isolated enzymes

key_finding.requirement: Both TrnC and TrnD required simultaneously for complete modification
key_finding.substrate_modification: Complete modification of TrnA and TrnB only achieved with TrnC/TrnD co-incubation

complex_formation.description: TrnC and TrnD form heterodimeric protein complex
complex_formation.binding_targets: Binds to either TrnA or TrnB precursor peptides
complex_formation.binding_affinity: Low micromolar affinity (typical for RiPP biosynthetic systems)

experimental_approach.method: In vitro activity assays with TrnC/TrnD heterodimers
experimental_approach.modification_strategy: Replaced one component with catalytically inactive variant
experimental_approach.catalytic_site_mutation: Mutations in conserved cysteine residues coordinating [4Fe–4S]1+ cluster

surprising_finding_2.observation: TrnC and TrnD function asymmetrically within the heterodimer
surprising_finding_2.trn_c_cluster_requirement: Only [4Fe–4S]1+ cluster of TrnC required for both TrnA and TrnB modification
surprising_finding_2.trn_d_cluster_role: Mutation of rSAM [4Fe–4S]1+ cluster in TrnD active site had NO apparent effect on TrnA/B modification
surprising_finding_2.implication: TrnD plays non-catalytic or primarily structural role in complex

structural_modeling_support.trn_d_role: Plays dominant role in binding to N-terminal leader peptide recognition element (RRE) of TrnA/B
structural_modeling_support.trn_c_role: rSAM active site has greater access to core peptide modification sites on C-terminus

rrE_background.traditional_role: Precursor peptide binding in RiPP systems
rrE_background.known_examples: Found in large number of RiPP biosynthetic enzymes

trn_c_rrE.structure: Canonical RRE with intact winged helix-turn-helix

trn_d_rrE.structure: Modified RRE with intact β-sheet ('wing') but distorted helical bundle

surprising_finding_3.observation: RREs of TrnC and TrnD interact extensively to form heterodimeric interface
surprising_finding_3.novel_function: New role for RRE in RiPP enzyme dimerization
surprising_finding_3.binding_contribution: Heterodimerization interface mediated by RRE-RRE interactions

validation_experiments.approach: Deletion of RREs coupled with binding studies
validation_experiments.result: >40-fold decrease in binding constant for TrnC/D heterodimerization

trn_a_core_peptide_binding.primary_interaction: Binds into catalytic core of TrnC

af3_structural_predictions.rSAM_cluster_locations: Approximate location of [4Fe–4S]1+ clusters bound by TrnC and TrnD indicated in model

both_rrE_wings_exposed.observation: Both 'wings' of TrnC and TrnD RREs exposed at protein surface
both_rrE_wings_exposed.apparent_availability: Appear available for precursor peptide interaction

preferential_binding.finding: TrnA and TrnB leader peptides predicted to interact preferentially with TrnD RRE
preferential_binding.confidence: Low confidence scores in AF3 model
preferential_binding.implication: Specific recognition mechanism for substrate targeting

well_characterized_two_component_systems.characteristics: Multiple precursor genes modified by same enzyme set

traditional_two_component_examples.mechanism: Each precursor peptide modified by dedicated synthetase

thuricin_cd_uniqueness.observation: Prior to Zhang et al. study, two-component sactipeptides were unknown

rSAM_enzyme_catalytic_versatility.description: rSAM enzymes comprise catalytically versatile superfamily
rSAM_enzyme_catalytic_versatility.abundance: Widespread in RiPP biosynthetic gene clusters
rSAM_enzyme_catalytic_versatility.mechanism: Utilize reduced [4Fe–4S]1+ cluster to generate 5'-deoxyadenosyl radical from SAM

challenge_to_predictability.issue: Highly dynamic nature of RiPP precursor peptides (typically intrinsically disordered)
complicating_factors: Structural plasticity of enzyme-peptide binding interactions
Relaxed substrate specificity of RiPP biosynthetic enzymes
Functional versatility linked to intrinsic structural dynamics

computational_prediction_limitations.finding: Functional versatility of RiPP biosynthetic enzymes linked to dynamics
computational_prediction_limitations.consequence: Structure-function relationships in RiPP biosynthesis remain challenging to computationally predict
computational_prediction_limitations.necessity: Detailed investigation of individual systems required

engineering_implications.challenge: Biophysical interactions in RiPP pathways vastly different across RiPP family
engineering_implications.requirement: Detailed mechanistic and structural studies needed for engineering applications
engineering_implications.context: Critical for understanding molecular basis of RiPP biosynthetic fidelity

key_scientific_insights: Obligatory heterodimeric enzyme complexes can catalyze RiPP biosynthesis
Asymmetric enzyme functions within complexes expand catalytic possibilities
RREs have novel roles beyond precursor peptide binding - enzyme-enzyme interactions
Two-component sactipeptides exist (previously thought limited to lanthipeptides)
Biosynthetic pathways defy simple predictions from sequence information
Enzyme dynamics and flexibility critical to substrate specificity and modification
Structure-function relationships in RiPP systems require experimental characterization

research_implications.design_predictions: Structures and functional properties of RiPPs cannot be predicted solely from gene sequence
research_implications.discovery_needs: Detailed investigation essential before engineering applications
research_implications.future_surprises: Many more biosynthetic surprises likely as community digs deeper into molecular logic
research_implications.engineering_caution: Calls for caution when making assumptions about versatile biosynthetic pathways
